read-your-writes.\n162\t#[tracing::instrument(skip(state, headers, body))]\n163\t#[axum::debug_handler]\n164\tasync fn search_handler(\n165\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n166\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n167\t    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n168\t    headers: HeaderMap,\n169\t    Json(body): Json<SearchRequestBody>,\n170\t) -> Response<Body> {\n171\t    let start = Instant::now();\n172\t    let client_requested_score = body.ranking_score.unwrap_or(false);\n173\t\n174\t    // Extract session ID from request extensions (set by session_pinning_middleware)\n175\t    let sid = session_id.map(|ext| ext.0).filter(|s| !s.as_str().is_empty());\n176\t\n177\t    // TODO: Extract source IP from headers - need to add back HeaderMap extraction\n178\t    let source_ip = \"unknown\".to_string();\n179\t\n180\t    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n181\t    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n182\t        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n183\t        Err(e) => {\n184\t            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n185\t            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n186\t        }\n187\t    };\n188\t\n189\t    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n190\t    if backend == \"redis\" {\n191\t        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n192\t            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n193\t                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n194\t                    if !allowed {\n195\t                        warn!(\n196\t                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n197\t                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n198\t                        );\n199\t                        return Response::builder()\n200\t                .status(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS)\n201\t                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n202\t                .unwrap();\n203\t                    }\n204\t                    // Allowed, proceed\n205\t                }\n206\t                Err(e) => {\n207\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n208\t                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n209\t                }\n210\t            }\n211\t        }\n212\t    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n213\t        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n214\t            &source_ip,\n215\t            limit,\n216\t            window_seconds * 1000,\n217\t        );\n218\t        if !allowed {\n219\t            warn!(\n220\t                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n221\t                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n222\t            );\n223\t            return Response::builder()\n224\t                .status(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS)\n225\t                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n226\t                .unwrap();\n227\t        }\n228\t    }\n229\t\n230\t    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n231\t    // Aliases are resolved at routing time; resolved index is used for scatter planning\n232\t    let (effective_index, resolved_targets) = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n233\t        let targets = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n234\t        state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n235\t        if targets != vec![index.clone()] {\n236\t            // It's an alias\n237\t            (index.clone(), targets)\n238\t        } else {\n239\t            // Not an alias - use index directly\n240\t            (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n241\t        }\n242\t    } else {\n243\t        // Aliases disabled - use index directly\n244\t        (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n245\t    };\n246\t\n247\t    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n248\t    let (pinned_group, strategy_label) = if let Some(ref sid) = sid {\n249\t        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid.as_str()).await {\n250\t            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n251\t            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n252\t            match strategy {\n253\t                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n254\t                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n255\t                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n256\t                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n257\t                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n258\t                        sid.as_str(),\n259\t                        &state.task_registry,\n260\t                        max_wait,\n261\t                    ).await {\n262\t                        Ok(true) => {\n263\t                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n264\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n265\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n266\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n267\t                            (None, Some(\"block_success\"))\n268\t                        }\n269\t                        Ok(false) => {\n270\t                            // Write failed, clear pin\n271\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n272\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n273\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n274\t                            (None, Some(\"block_failed\"))\n275\t                        }\n276\t                        Err(_) => {\n277\t                            // Timeout - still route to pinned group (best effort)\n278\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n279\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n280\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n281\t                            (Some(group), Some(\"block_timeout\"))\n282\t                        }\n283\t                    }\n284\t                }\n285\t                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n286\t                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n287\t                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n288\t                    (Some(group), Some(\"route_pin\"))\n289\t                }\n290\t            }\n291\t        } else {\n292\t            // No pending write - normal routing\n293\t            (None, None)\n294\t        }\n295\t    } else {\n296\t        // No session header - normal routing\n297\t        (None, None)\n298\t    };\n299\t\n300\t    // Log session pinning decision for observability\n301\t    if let Some(label) = strategy_label {\n302\t        debug!(\n303\t            session_id = ?sid,\n304\t            pinned_group = pinned_group,\n305\t            strategy = label,\n306\t            \"session pinning applied\"\n307\t        );\n308\t    }\n309\t\n310\t    // Query coalescing (plan §13.10): Check for identical in-flight queries\n311\t    // Skip for multi-target aliases (each target is different)\n312\t    if state.config.query_coalescing.enabled && resolved_targets.len() == 1 {\n313\t        // Build fingerprint from canonicalized query body + index + settings version\n314\t        let settings_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n315\t        let query_json = serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap_or_default();\n316\t        let fingerprint = QueryFingerprint {\n317\t            index: effective_index.clone(),\n318\t            query_json,\n319\t            settings_version,\n320\t        };\n321\t\n322\t        // Try to coalesce with an existing in-flight query\n323\t        if let Some(mut rx) = state.query_coalescer.try_coalesce(fingerprint.clone()).await {\n324\t            // Successfully subscribed to an in-flight query - wait for its result\n325\t            state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_hits();\n326\t            debug!(\n327\t                index = %effective_index,\n328\t                \"query coalesced: waiting for in-flight query result\"\n329\t            );\n330\t\n331\t            // Wait for the response (with timeout matching the scatter timeout)\n332\t            let timeout = Duration::from_millis(state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms);\n333\t            let response_bytes = tokio::time::timeout(timeout, async move {\n334\t                rx.recv().await\n335\t            }).await;\n336\t\n337\t            match response_bytes {\n338\t                Ok(Ok(response_bytes)) => {\n339\t                    // Received response from coalesced query\n340\t                    let response_body: Value = match serde_json::from_slice(&response_bytes) {\n341\t                        Ok(v) => v,\n342\t                        Err(e) => {\n343\t                            error!(error = %e, \"failed to deserialize coalesced query response\");\n344\t                            return Response::builder()\n345\t                                .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n346\t                                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n347\t                                .unwrap();\n348\t                        }\n349\t                    };\n350\t\n351\t                    // Build response with appropriate headers\n352\t                    let mut response = Response::builder()\n353\t                        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n354\t                        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n355\t\n356\t                    // Add settings headers\n357\t                    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(&effective_index).await {\n358\t                        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n359\t                    }\n360\t                    if settings_version > 0 {\n361\t                        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", settings_version.to_string());\n362\t                    }\n363\t\n364\t                    let response = response\n365\t                        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&response_body).unwrap()))\n366\t                        .unwrap();\n367\t\n368\t                    tracing::info!(\n369\t                        target: \"miroir.search_coalesced\",\n370\t                        index = %effective_index,\n371\t                        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n372\t                        \"coalesced search completed\"\n373\t                    );\n374\t\n375\t                    return response;\n376\t                }\n377\t                Ok(Err(_)) => {\n378\t                    // Channel closed without receiving response - proceed with normal scatter\n379\t                    debug!(\"coalesced query channel closed, proceeding with normal scatter\");\n380\t                }\n381\t                Err(_) => {\n382\t                    // Timeout waiting for coalesced query - proceed with normal scatter\n383\t                    debug!(\"timeout waiting for coalesced query, proceeding with normal scatter\");\n384\t                }\n385\t            }\n386\t        }\n387\t    }\n388\t\n389\t    // Extract X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header (plan §13.5)\n390\t    // Extract early for multi-target search path\n391\t    let min_settings_version = headers\n392\t        .get(\"X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version\")\n393\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n394\t        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n395\t\n396\t    // Handle multi-target alias fanout (plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17)\n397\t    // Multi-target aliases (ILM read_alias) require fanning out to all targets\n398\t    // and merging results by _rankingScore\n399\t    if resolved_targets.len() > 1 {\n400\t        // Need to create a new Extension wrapper for the nested call\n401\t        // Clone the Arc for the multi-target search\n402\t        return search_multi_targets(\n403\t            resolved_targets,\n404\t            body,\n405\t            Extension(state.clone()),\n406\t            sid.map(|s| s.as_str().to_string()),\n407\t            client_requested_score,\n408\t            min_settings_version,\n409\t        ).await;\n410\t    }\n411\t\n412\t    // Get the scoped key for this index (plan §13.21).\n413\t    // If a scoped key exists, use primary_key (or previous_key during rotation overlap).\n414\t    // If no scoped key exists yet, fall back to node_master_key for initial setup.\n415\t    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n416\t        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(&effective_index) {\n417\t            // Refresh scoped-key beacon so the rotation leader knows this pod is serving\n418\t            // requests for this index at the current generation (plan §13.21).\n419\t            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n420\t                &state.pod_id,\n421\t                &index,\n422\t                sk.generation,\n423\t            );\n424\t\n425\t            // Use primary_key; previous_key is the overlap fallback (both are valid in Meilisearch)\n426\t            sk.primary_key\n427\t        } else {\n428\t            // No scoped key yet — fall back to node_master_key for initial setup\n429\t            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n430\t        }\n431\t    } else {\n432\t        // No Redis store — fall back to node_master_key (single-pod dev mode)\n433\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n434\t    };\n435\t\n436\t    // Extract X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header (plan §13.5)\n437\t    let min_settings_version = headers\n438\t        .get(\"X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version\")\n439\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n440\t        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n441\t\n442\t    // Use live topology from shared state (updated by health checker)\n443\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n444\t    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n445\t        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n446\t        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n447\t        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n448\t        _ => return Response::builder()\n449\t            .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n450\t            .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n451\t            .unwrap(),\n452\t    };\n453\t\n454\t    // Plan scatter using live topology (span for plan construction)\n455\t    let plan = {\n456\t        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n457\t            \"scatter_plan\",\n458\t            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n459\t            shards = state.config.shards,\n460\t            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n461\t            min_settings_version,\n462\t            pinned_group = ?pinned_group,\n463\t            strategy = %state.config.replica_selection.strategy,\n464\t        ).entered();\n465\t\n466\t        // Determine if we should use adaptive selection\n467\t        let use_adaptive = state.config.replica_selection.strategy == \"adaptive\";\n468\t        let replica_selector_ref = if use_adaptive {\n469\t            Some(state.replica_selector.as_ref())\n470\t        } else {\n471\t            None\n472\t        };\n473\t\n474\t        // Session pinning: if pinned_group is set, use group-specific planning\n475\t        if let Some(group) = pinned_group {\n476\t            match plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n477\t                &topo,\n478\t                0,\n479\t                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n480\t                state.config.shards,\n481\t                group,\n482\t                replica_selector_ref,\n483\t            ).await {\n484\t                Some(p) => p,\n485\t                None => {\n486\t                    // Pinned group not available - fall back to normal planning\n487\t                    // This can happen if the pinned group has failed\n488\t                    warn!(\n489\t                        pinned_group = group,\n490\t                        \"pinned group unavailable, falling back to normal routing\"\n491\t                    );\n492\t                    plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, replica_selector_ref).await\n493\t                }\n494\t            }\n495\t        } else if let Some(floor) = min_settings_version {\n496\t            // If client provided a min settings version floor, use version-filtered planning\n497\t            // Clone the settings broadcast for version checking\n498\t            let settings_broadcast = state.settings_broadcast.clone();\n499\t            let plan_result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n500\t                &topo,\n501\t                0,\n502\t                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n503\t                state.config.shards,\n504\t                &index,\n505\t                floor,\n506\t                &move |idx, node_id| {\n507\t                    // Use a blocking task wrapper since we're in a sync context\n508\t                    let sb = settings_broadcast.clone();\n509\t                    let idx = idx.to_string();\n510\t                    let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n511\t                    tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n512\t                        tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n513\t                            sb.node_version(&idx, &node_id).await\n514\t                        })\n515\t                    })\n516\t                },\n517\t                replica_selector_ref,\n518\t            ).await;\n519\t\n520\t            match plan_result {\n521\t                Some(p) => p,\n522\t                None => {\n523\t                    // No covering set could be assembled after filtering by version floor\n524\t                    let _err = MeilisearchError::new(\n525\t                        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n526\t                        format!(\n527\t                            \"no covering set available for settings version floor {} on index '{}'\",\n528\t                            floor, index\n529\t                        ),\n530\t                    );\n531\t                    return Response::builder()\n532\t                        .status(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)\n533\t                        .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n534\t                        .unwrap();\n535\t                }\n536\t            }\n537\t        } else {\n538\t            // No version floor requested, use normal planning\n539\t            plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, replica_selector_ref).await\n540\t        }\n541\t    };\n542\t    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n543\t\n544\t    // Record scatter fan-out size before executing\n545\t    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n546\t\n547\t    // Build search request\n548\t    // Clone facets for fingerprinting before moving into SearchRequest\n549\t    let facets_clone = body.facets.clone();\n550\t    let rest_body = body.rest.clone(); // Clone before body is partially moved\n551\t    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n552\t        index_uid: effective_index.clone(),\n553\t        query: body.q,\n554\t        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n555\t        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n556\t        filter: body.filter,\n557\t        facets: body.facets,\n558\t        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n559\t        body: rest_body,\n560\t        global_idf: None,\n561\t    };\n562\t\n563\t    // Create node client with the scoped key (or node_master_key as fallback)\n564\t    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n565\t        search_key,\n566\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n567\t    ));\n568\t    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone(), None);\n569\t\n570\t    // Use score-based merge strategy (OP#4: requires global IDF)\n571\t    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n572\t\n573\t    // Register for query coalescing (plan §13.10) - after try_coalesce, before scatter\n574\t    // Only register if coalescing is enabled and this is a single-target query\n575\t    let (tx, fingerprint) = if state.config.query_coalescing.enabled && resolved_targets.len() == 1 {\n576\t        let settings_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n577\t        // Reconstruct body for fingerprinting (use cloned facets)\n578\t        let fingerprint_body = SearchRequestBody {\n579\t            q: search_req.query.clone(),\n580\t            offset: Some(search_req.offset),\n581\t            limit: Some(search_req.limit),\n582\t            filter: search_req.filter.clone(),\n583\t            facets: facets_clone,\n584\t            ranking_score: Some(search_req.ranking_score),\n585\t            rest: search_req.body.clone(),\n586\t        };\n587\t        let query_json = serde_json::to_string(&fingerprint_body).unwrap_or_default();\n588\t        let fp = QueryFingerprint {\n589\t            index: effective_index.clone(),\n590\t            query_json,\n591\t            settings_version,\n592\t        };\n593\t\n594\t        match state.query_coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await {\n595\t            Ok(broadcast_tx) => {\n596\t                state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_subscribers(1); // First subscriber = the query itself\n597\t                (Some(broadcast_tx), Some(fp))\n598\t            }\n599\t            Err(_) => {\n600\t                // Failed to register (too many pending queries) - proceed without coalescing\n601\t                debug!(\"too many pending queries, proceeding without coalescing registration\");\n602\t                (None, None)\n603\t            }\n604\t        }\n605\t    } else {\n606\t        (None, None)\n607\t    };\n608\t\n609\t    // Execute DFS query-then-fetch\n610\t    let mut result = match dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n611\t        plan,\n612\t        &client,\n613\t        search_req,\n614\t        &topo,\n615\t        policy,\n616\t        &strategy,\n617\t    )\n618\t    .await\n619\t    {\n620\t        Ok(r) => r,\n621\t        Err(e) => {\n622\t            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"search failed\");\n623\t            return Response::builder()\n624\t                .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n625\t                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n626\t                .unwrap();\n627\t        }\n628\t    };\n629\t\n630\t    // Drop topology lock before building response\n631\t    drop(topo);\n632\t\n633\t    // Strip internal fields from hits\n634\t    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n635\t        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n636\t    }\n637\t\n638\t    // Build response body\n639\t    let response_body = serde_json::json!({\n640\t        \"hits\": result.hits,\n641\t        \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n642\t        \"processingTimeMs\": result.processing_time_ms,\n643\t    });\n644\t\n645\t    // Only include facetDistribution if facets were requested\n646\t    let mut body = response_body.clone();\n647\t    if let Some(facets) = &result.facet_distribution {\n648\t        body[\"facetDistribution\"] = serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n649\t    }\n650\t\n651\t    // Broadcast result to coalesced queries (plan §13.10)\n652\t    if let (Some(broadcast_tx), Some(fp)) = (tx, fingerprint) {\n653\t        let response_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(&body).unwrap_or_default();\n654\t        let subscriber_count = broadcast_tx.receiver_count();\n655\t        if subscriber_count > 1 {\n656\t            // There are other queries waiting for this result - broadcast to them\n657\t            state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_subscribers(subscriber_count as u64 - 1);\n658\t            let _ = broadcast_tx.send(response_bytes.clone());\n659\t            debug!(\n660\t                index = %effective_index,\n661\t                subscribers = subscriber_count - 1,\n662\t                \"broadcast search result to coalesced queries\"\n663\t            );\n664\t        }\n665\t        // Unregister the query after broadcasting\n666\t        state.query_coalescer.unregister(&fp).await;\n667\t    }\n668\t\n669\t    // Build response with optional headers\n670\t    let mut response = Response::builder()\n671\t        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n672\t        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n673\t\n674\t    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent header if a broadcast is in flight (plan §13.5)\n675\t    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(&effective_index).await {\n676\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n677\t    }\n678\t\n679\t    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Version header if we have a version for this index\n680\t    let current_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n681\t    if current_version > 0 {\n682\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", current_version.to_string());\n683\t    }\n684\t\n685\t    if result.degraded {\n686\t        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n687\t    }\n688\t\n689\t    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n690\t        let mut sorted_shards = result.failed_shards.clone();\n691\t        sorted_shards.sort();\n692\t        let shard_ids = sorted_shards.iter()\n693\t            .map(|id| id.to_string())\n694\t            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n695\t            .join(\",\");\n696\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids));\n697\t    } else if result.degraded {\n698\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", \"partial\");\n699\t    }\n700\t\n701\t    let response = response\n702\t        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap()))\n703\t        .unwrap();\n704\t\n705\t    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n706\t    // request_id and pod_id are included from the middleware span via\n707\t    // .with_current_span(true) on the JSON subscriber layer.\n708\t    tracing::info!(\n709\t        target: \"miroir.search\",\n710\t        index = %effective_index,\n711\t        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n712\t        node_count = node_count,\n713\t        estimated_hits = result.estimated_total_hits,\n714\t        degraded = result.degraded,\n715\t        \"search completed\"\n716\t    );\n717\t\n718\t    response\n719\t}\n720\t\n721\t/// Search multiple target indexes (for multi-target aliases, plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17).\n722\t///\n723\t/// Fans out the search to all target indexes and merges results by _rankingScore.\n724\t/// Used by ILM read_alias queries.\n725\tasync fn search_multi_targets(\n726\t    targets: Vec<String>,\n727\t    body: SearchRequestBody,\n728\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n729\t    session_id: Option<String>,\n730\t    client_requested_score: bool,\n731\t    min_settings_version: Option<u64>,\n732\t) -> Response<Body> {\n733\t    let start = Instant::now();\n734\t\n735\t    // TODO: Extract source IP from headers\n736\t    let source_ip = \"unknown\".to_string();\n737\t\n738\t    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n739\t    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n740\t        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n741\t        Err(e) => {\n742\t            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n743\t            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n744\t        }\n745\t    };\n746\t\n747\t    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n748\t    if backend == \"redis\" {\n749\t        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n750\t            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n751\t                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n752\t                    if !allowed {\n753\t                        warn!(\n754\t                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n755\t                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n756\t                        );\n757\t                        return Response::builder()\n758\t                .status(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS)\n759\t                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n760\t                .unwrap();\n761\t                    }\n762\t                    // Allowed, proceed\n763\t                }\n764\t                Err(e) => {\n765\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n766\t                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n767\t                }\n768\t            }\n769\t        }\n770\t    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n771\t        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n772\t            &source_ip,\n773\t            limit,\n774\t            window_seconds * 1000,\n775\t        );\n776\t        if !allowed {\n777\t            warn!(\n778\t                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n779\t                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n780\t            );\n781\t            return Response::builder()\n782\t                .status(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS)\n783\t                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n784\t                .unwrap();\n785\t        }\n786\t    }\n787\t\n788\t    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n789\t    let (pinned_group, _strategy_label) = if let Some(sid) = session_id {\n790\t        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid.as_str()).await {\n791\t            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n792\t            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n793\t            match strategy {\n794\t                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n795\t                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n796\t                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n797\t                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n798\t                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n799\t                        sid.as_str(),\n800\t                        &state.task_registry,\n801\t                        max_wait,\n802\t                    ).await {\n803\t                        Ok(true) => {\n804\t                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n805\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n806\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n807\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n808\t                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n809\t                        }\n810\t                        Ok(false) => {\n811\t                            // Write failed, clear pin\n812\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n813\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n814\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n815\t                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n816\t                        }\n817\t                        Err(_) => {\n818\t                            // Timeout - route to pinned group (best effort)\n819\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n820\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n821\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n822\t                            (Some(group), None::<u64>)\n823\t                        }\n824\t                    }\n825\t                }\n826\t                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n827\t                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n828\t                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n829\t                    (Some(group), None::<u64>)\n830\t                }\n831\t            }\n832\t        } else {\n833\t            // No pending write - normal routing\n834\t            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n835\t        }\n836\t    } else {\n837\t        // No session header - normal routing\n838\t        (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n839\t    };\n840\t\n841\t    // For multi-target aliases, we use the first target for settings/scoped key\n842\t    // All targets should have compatible settings (managed by ILM)\n843\t    let primary_target = match targets.first() {\n844\t        Some(t) => t,\n845\t        None => {\n846\t            error!(\"multi-target alias has no targets\");\n847\t            return Response::builder()\n848\t                .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n849\t                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n850\t                .unwrap();\n851\t        }\n852\t    };\n853\t\n854\t    // Get the scoped key for the primary target\n855\t    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n856\t        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(primary_target) {\n857\t            // Refresh scoped-key beacon\n858\t            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n859\t                &state.pod_id,\n860\t                primary_target,\n861\t                sk.generation,\n862\t            );\n863\t            sk.primary_key\n864\t        } else {\n865\t            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n866\t        }\n867\t    } else {\n868\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n869\t    };\n870\t\n871\t    // Use live topology from shared state\n872\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n873\t    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n874\t        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n875\t        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n876\t        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n877\t        _ => return Response::builder()\n878\t            .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n879\t            .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n880\t            .unwrap(),\n881\t    };\n882\t\n883\t    // Plan scatter for primary target (for ILM \"scatter_plan_multi_target\",\n888\t            primary_target = %primary_target,\n889\t            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n890\t            shards = state.config.shards,\n891\t            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n892\t            min_settings_version,\n893\t            pinned_group = ?pinned_group,\n894\t            target_count = {\n906\t                Some(p) => p,\n907\t                None => {\n908\t                    warn!(\n909\t                        pinned_group = group,\n910\t                        \"pinned group   }\n1044\t\n1045\t    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n1046\t        let mut sorted_shards = result.failed_shards.clone();\n1047\t        sorted_shards.sort();\n1048\t dfs_query_then_fetch_search, plan_search_scatter, plan_search_scatter_for_group, plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor, SearchRequest, NodeClient,\n};\nuse warn};\n\nuse crate::routes::admin_endpoints::{AppState, parse_rate_limit};\n\n/// Metrics observer for replica selection events.\n///\n/// Reports selection scores and exploration events to Prometheus.\nstruct MetricsObserver {\n    metrics: crate::middleware::Metrics,\n}\n\nimpl SelectionObserver for MetricsObserver {\n    fn report_selection(&self, node_id: &str, score: f64) {\n        self.metrics.set_replica_selection_score(node_id, score);\n    }\n\n    fn report_exploration(&self) {\n        self.metrics.inc_replica_selection_exploration();\n    }\n}\n\n/// Hash a value for logging (obfuscates sensitive data like IPs).\nfn hash_for_log(value: &str) -> String {\n    use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n    let mut hasher = std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher::new();\n    value.hash(&mut hasher);\n    format!(\"{:016x}\", hasher.finish())\n}\n\n/// Node client implementation using the HTTP client.\n///\n/// Wraps each node call with Prometheus metrics recording for\n/// `miroir_node_request_duration_seconds` and `miroir_node_errors_total`.\npub struct ProxyNodeClient {\n    client: Arc<crate::client::HttpClient>,\n    metrics: crate::middleware::Metrics,\n    replica_selector: Option<Arc<miroir_core::replica_selection::ReplicaSelector>>,\n}\n\nimpl ProxyNodeClient {\n    pub fn new(\n        client: Arc<crate::client::HttpClient>,\n        metrics: crate::middleware::Metrics,\n        replica_selector: Option<Arc<miroir_core::replica_selection::ReplicaSelector>>,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self { client, metrics, replica_selector }\n    }\n}\n\n#[allow(async_fn_in_trait)]\nimpl NodeClient for ProxyNodeClient {\n    async fn search_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &miroir_core::topology::NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &SearchRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, miroir_core::scatter::NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let result = self.client.search_node(node, address, request).await;\n        let elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n        self.metrics.record_node_request_duration(node.as_str(), \"search\", elapsed);\n        if let Err(ref e) = result {\n            self.metrics.inc_node_errors(node.as_str(), error_label(e));\n        }\n        result\n    }\n\n    async fn preflight_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &miroir_core::topology::NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &miroir_core::scatter::PreflightRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<miroir_core::scatter::PreflightResponse, miroir_core::scatter::NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let result = self.client.preflight_node(node, address, request).await;\n        let elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n        self.metrics.record_node_request_duration(node.as_str(), \"preflight\", elapsed);\n        if let Err(ref e) = result {\n            self.metrics.inc_node_errors(node.as_str(), error_label(e));\n        }\n        result\n    }\n}\n\nfn error_label(e: &miroir_core::scatter::NodeError) -> &'static str {\n    match e {\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::NetworkError(_) => \"network\",\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::HttpError { .. } => \"http\",\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::Timeout => \"timeout\",\n    }\n}\n\npub fn router<S>() -> axum::Router<S>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n{\n    axum::Router::new()\n        .route(\"/:index\", axum::routing::post(search_handler))\n}\n\n/// Search request body.\n#[derive(Deserialize, Serialize)]\nstruct SearchRequestBody {\n    q: Option<String>,\n    offset: Option<usize>,\n    limit: Option<usize>,\n    filter: Option<Value>,\n    facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n    #[serde(rename = \"rankingScore\")]\n    ranking_score: Option<bool>,\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    rest: Value,\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Debug for SearchRequestBody {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        f.debug_struct(\"SearchRequestBody\")\n            .field(\"q\", &\"[redacted]\")\n            .field(\"offset\", &self.offset)\n            .field(\"limit\", &self.limit)\n            .field(\"filter\", &\"[redacted]\")\n            .field(\"facets\", &self.facets)\n            .field(\"ranking_score\", &self.ranking_score)\n            .finish_non_exhaustive()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Search handler with DFS global-IDF preflight (OP#4).\n///\n/// This handler implements the `dfs_query_then_fetch` pattern:\n/// 1. **Preflight phase**: Send term-frequency query to all shards, aggregate\n///    global document frequencies at the coordinator.\n/// 2. **Search phase**: Send the search query with global IDF attached so that\n///    scoring uses corpus-wide statistics instead of per-shard local IDF.\n///\n/// Returns `X-Miroir-Degraded: shards=X,Y,Z` header when any shards are unavailable.\n/// Strips `_miroir_shard` from all hits; strips `_rankingScore` unless client\n/// explicitly requested it.\n///\n/// Session pinning (plan §13.6): If `X-Miroir-Session` header is present and\n/// the session has a pending write, routes to the pinned group for read-your-writes.\n#[tracing::instrument(skip(state, headers, body))]\n#[axum::debug_handler]\nasync fn search_handler(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n    headers: HeaderMap,\n    Json(body): Json<SearchRequestBody>,\n) -> Response<Body> {\n    let start = Instant::now();\n    let client_requested_score = body.ranking_score.unwrap_or(false);\n\n    // Extract session ID from request extensions (set by session_pinning_middleware)\n    let sid = session_id.map(|ext| ext.0).filter(|s| !s.as_str().is_empty());\n\n    // TODO: Extract source IP from headers - need to add back HeaderMap extraction\n    let source_ip = \"unknown\".to_string();\n\n    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n        Err(e) => {\n            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n        }\n    };\n\n    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n    if backend == \"redis\" {\n        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n                    if !allowed {\n                        warn!(\n                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n                        );\n                        return Response::builder()\n                .status(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS)\n                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                .unwrap();\n                    }\n                    // Allowed, proceed\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n            &source_ip,\n            limit,\n            window_seconds * 1000,\n        );\n        if !allowed {\n            warn!(\n                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n            );\n            return Response::builder()\n                .status(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS)\n                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                .unwrap();\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n    // Aliases are resolved at routing time; resolved index is used for scatter planning\n    let (effective_index, resolved_targets) = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        let targets = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n        state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n        if targets != vec![index.clone()] {\n            // It's an alias\n            (index.clone(), targets)\n        } else {\n            // Not an alias - use index directly\n            (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n        }\n    } else {\n        // Aliases disabled - use index directly\n        (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n    };\n\n    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n    let (pinned_group, strategy_label) = if let Some(ref sid) = sid {\n        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid.as_str()).await {\n            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n            match strategy {\n                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n                        sid.as_str(),\n                        &state.task_registry,\n                        max_wait,\n                    ).await {\n                        Ok(true) => {\n                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n                            (None, Some(\"block_success\"))\n                        }\n                        Ok(false) => {\n                            // Write failed, clear pin\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (None, Some(\"block_failed\"))\n                        }\n                        Err(_) => {\n                            // Timeout - still route to pinned group (best effort)\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (Some(group), Some(\"block_timeout\"))\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n                    (Some(group), Some(\"route_pin\"))\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // No pending write - normal routing\n            (None, None)\n        }\n    } else {\n        // No session header - normal routing\n        (None, None)\n    };\n\n    // Log session pinning decision for observability\n    if let Some(label) = strategy_label {\n        debug!(\n            session_id = ?sid,\n            pinned_group = pinned_group,\n            strategy = label,\n            \"session pinning applied\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Query coalescing (plan §13.10): Check for identical in-flight queries\n    // Skip for multi-target aliases (each target is different)\n    if state.config.query_coalescing.enabled && resolved_targets.len() == 1 {\n        // Build fingerprint from canonicalized query body + index + settings version\n        let settings_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n        let query_json = serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap_or_default();\n        let fingerprint = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: effective_index.clone(),\n            query_json,\n            settings_version,\n        };\n\n        // Try to coalesce with an existing in-flight query\n        if let Some(mut rx) = state.query_coalescer.try_coalesce(fingerprint.clone()).await {\n            // Successfully subscribed to an in-flight query - wait for its result\n            state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_hits();\n            debug!(\n                index = %effective_index,\n                \"query coalesced: waiting for in-flight query result\"\n            );\n\n            // Wait for the response (with timeout matching the scatter timeout)\n            let timeout = Duration::from_millis(state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms);\n            let response_bytes = tokio::time::timeout(timeout, async move {\n                rx.recv().await\n            }).await;\n\n            match response_bytes {\n                Ok(Ok(response_bytes)) => {\n                    // Received response from coalesced query\n                    let response_body: Value = match serde_json::from_slice(&response_bytes) {\n                        Ok(v) => v,\n                        Err(e) => {\n                            error!(error = %e, \"failed to deserialize coalesced query response\");\n                            return Response::builder()\n                                .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n                                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                                .unwrap();\n                        }\n                    };\n\n                    // Build response with appropriate headers\n                    let mut response = Response::builder()\n                        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n                        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n\n                    // Add settings headers\n                    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(&effective_index).await {\n                        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n                    }\n                    if settings_version > 0 {\n                        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", settings_version.to_string());\n                    }\n\n                    let response = response\n                        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&response_body).unwrap()))\n                        .unwrap();\n\n                    tracing::info!(\n                        target: \"miroir.search_coalesced\",\n                        index = %effective_index,\n                        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n                        \"coalesced search completed\"\n                    );\n\n                    return response;\n                }\n                Ok(Err(_)) => {\n                    // Channel closed without receiving response - proceed with normal scatter\n                    debug!(\"coalesced query channel closed, proceeding with normal scatter\");\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    // Timeout waiting for coalesced query - proceed with normal scatter\n                    debug!(\"timeout waiting for coalesced query, proceeding with normal scatter\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Extract X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header (plan §13.5)\n    // Extract early for multi-target search path\n    let min_settings_version = headers\n        .get(\"X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n\n    // Handle multi-target alias fanout (plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17)\n    // Multi-target aliases (ILM read_alias) require fanning out to all targets\n    // and merging results by _rankingScore\n    if resolved_targets.len() > 1 {\n        // Need to create a new Extension wrapper for the nested call\n        // Clone the Arc for the multi-target search\n        return search_multi_targets(\n            resolved_targets,\n            body,\n            Extension(state.clone()),\n            sid.map(|s| s.as_str().to_string()),\n            client_requested_score,\n            min_settings_version,\n        ).await;\n    }\n\n    // Get the scoped key for this index (plan §13.21).\n    // If a scoped key exists, use primary_key (or previous_key during rotation overlap).\n    // If no scoped key exists yet, fall back to node_master_key for initial setup.\n    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(&effective_index) {\n            // Refresh scoped-key beacon so the rotation leader knows this pod is serving\n            // requests for this index at the current generation (plan §13.21).\n            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n                &state.pod_id,\n                &index,\n                sk.generation,\n            );\n\n            // Use primary_key; previous_key is the overlap fallback (both are valid in Meilisearch)\n            sk.primary_key\n        } else {\n            // No scoped key yet — fall back to node_master_key for initial setup\n            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        // No Redis store — fall back to node_master_key (single-pod dev mode)\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n    };\n\n    // Extract X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header (plan §13.5)\n    let min_settings_version = headers\n        .get(\"X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n\n    // Use live topology from shared state (updated by health checker)\n    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n        _ => return Response::builder()\n            .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n            .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n            .unwrap(),\n    };\n\n    // Plan scatter using live topology (span for plan construction)\n    let plan = {\n        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n            \"scatter_plan\",\n            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n            shards = state.config.shards,\n            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n            min_settings_version,\n            pinned_group = ?pinned_group,\n            strategy = %state.config.replica_selection.strategy,\n        ).entered();\n\n        // Determine if we should use adaptive selection\n        let use_adaptive = state.config.replica_selection.strategy == \"adaptive\";\n        let replica_selector_ref = if use_adaptive {\n            Some(state.replica_selector.as_ref())\n        } else {\n            None\n        };\n\n        // Session pinning: if pinned_group is set, use group-specific planning\n        if let Some(group) = pinned_group {\n            match plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                group,\n                replica_selector_ref,\n            ).await {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    // Pinned group not available - fall back to normal planning\n                    // This can happen if the pinned group has failed\n                    warn!(\n                        pinned_group = group,\n                        \"pinned group unavailable, falling back to normal routing\"\n                    );\n                    plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, replica_selector_ref).await\n                }\n            }\n        } else if let Some(floor) = min_settings_version {\n            // If client provided a min settings version floor, use version-filtered planning\n            // Clone the settings broadcast for version checking\n            let settings_broadcast = state.settings_broadcast.clone();\n            let plan_result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                &index,\n                floor,\n                &move |idx, node_id| {\n                    // Use a blocking task wrapper since we're in a sync context\n                    let sb = settings_broadcast.clone();\n                    let idx = idx.to_string();\n                    let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n                    tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n                        tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n                            sb.node_version(&idx, &node_id).await\n                        })\n                    })\n                },\n                replica_selector_ref,\n            ).await;\n\n            match plan_result {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    // No covering set could be assembled after filtering by version floor\n                    let _err = MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n                        format!(\n                            \"no covering set available for settings version floor {} on index '{}'\",\n                            floor, index\n                        ),\n                    );\n                    return Response::builder()\n                        .status(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)\n                        .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                        .unwrap();\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // No version floor requested, use normal planning\n            plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, replica_selector_ref).await\n        }\n    };\n    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n\n    // Record scatter fan-out size before executing\n    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n\n    // Build search request\n    // Clone facets for fingerprinting before moving into SearchRequest\n    let facets_clone = body.facets.clone();\n    let rest_body = body.rest.clone(); // Clone before body is partially moved\n    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: effective_index.clone(),\n        query: body.q,\n        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n        filter: body.filter,\n        facets: body.facets,\n        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n        body: rest_body,\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    // Create node client with the scoped key (or node_master_key as fallback)\n    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n        search_key,\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    ));\n    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone(), None);\n\n    // Use score-based merge strategy (OP#4: requires global IDF)\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n    // Register for query coalescing (plan §13.10) - after try_coalesce, before scatter\n    // Only register if coalescing is enabled and this is a single-target query\n    let (tx, fingerprint) = if state.config.query_coalescing.enabled && resolved_targets.len() == 1 {\n        let settings_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n        // Reconstruct body for fingerprinting (use cloned facets)\n        let fingerprint_body = SearchRequestBody {\n            q: search_req.query.clone(),\n            offset: Some(search_req.offset),\n            limit: Some(search_req.limit),\n            filter: search_req.filter.clone(),\n            facets: facets_clone,\n            ranking_score: Some(search_req.ranking_score),\n            rest: search_req.body.clone(),\n        };\n        let query_json = serde_json::to_string(&fingerprint_body).unwrap_or_default();\n        let fp = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: effective_index.clone(),\n            query_json,\n            settings_version,\n        };\n\n        match state.query_coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await {\n            Ok(broadcast_tx) => {\n                state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_subscribers(1); // First subscriber = the query itself\n                (Some(broadcast_tx), Some(fp))\n            }\n            Err(_) => {\n                // Failed to register (too many pending queries) - proceed without coalescing\n                debug!(\"too many pending queries, proceeding without coalescing registration\");\n                (None, None)\n            }\n        }\n    } else {\n        (None, None)\n    };\n\n    // Execute DFS query-then-fetch\n    let mut result = match dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        search_req,\n        &topo,\n        policy,\n        &strategy,\n    )\n    .await\n    {\n        Ok(r) => r,\n        Err(e) => {\n            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"search failed\");\n            return Response::builder()\n                .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                .unwrap();\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Drop topology lock before building response\n    drop(topo);\n\n    // Strip internal fields from hits\n    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n    }\n\n    // Build response body\n    let response_body = serde_json::json!({\n        \"hits\": result.hits,\n        \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n        \"processingTimeMs\": result.processing_time_ms,\n    });\n\n    // Only include facetDistribution if facets were requested\n    let mut body = response_body.clone();\n    if let Some(facets) = &result.facet_distribution {\n        body[\"facetDistribution\"] = serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    }\n\n    // Broadcast result to coalesced queries (plan §13.10)\n    if let (Some(broadcast_tx), Some(fp)) = (tx, fingerprint) {\n        let response_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(&body).unwrap_or_default();\n        let subscriber_count = broadcast_tx.receiver_count();\n        if subscriber_count > 1 {\n            // There are other queries waiting for this result - broadcast to them\n            state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_subscribers(subscriber_count as u64 - 1);\n            let _ = broadcast_tx.send(response_bytes.clone());\n            debug!(\n                index = %effective_index,\n                subscribers = subscriber_count - 1,\n                \"broadcast search result to coalesced queries\"\n            );\n        }\n        // Unregister the query after broadcasting\n        state.query_coalescer.unregister(&fp).await;\n    }\n\n    // Build response with optional headers\n    let mut response = Response::builder()\n        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n\n    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent header if a broadcast is in flight (plan §13.5)\n    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(&effective_index).await {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n    }\n\n    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Version header if we have a version for this index\n    let current_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n    if current_version > 0 {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", current_version.to_string());\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded {\n        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n        let mut sorted_shards = result.failed_shards.clone();\n        sorted_shards.sort();\n        let shard_ids = sorted_shards.iter()\n            .map(|id| id.to_string())\n            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n            .join(\",\");\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids));\n    } else if result.degraded {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", \"partial\");\n    }\n\n    let response = response\n        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap()))\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n    // request_id and pod_id are included from the middleware span via\n    // .with_current_span(true) on the JSON subscriber layer.\n    tracing::info!(\n        target: \"miroir.search\",\n        index = %effective_index,\n        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n        node_count = node_count,\n        estimated_hits = result.estimated_total_hits,\n        degraded = result.degraded,\n        \"search completed\"\n    );\n\n    response\n}\n\n/// Search multiple target indexes (for multi-target aliases, plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17).\n///\n/// Fans out the search to all target indexes and merges results by _rankingScore.\n/// Used by ILM read_alias queries.\nasync fn search_multi_targets(\n    targets: Vec<String>,\n    body: SearchRequestBody,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n    client_requested_score: bool,\n    min_settings_version: Option<u64>,\n) -> Response<Body> {\n    let start = Instant::now();\n\n    // TODO: Extract source IP from headers\n    let source_ip = \"unknown\".to_string();\n\n    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n        Err(e) => {\n            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n        }\n    };\n\n    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n    if backend == \"redis\" {\n        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n                    if !allowed {\n                        warn!(\n                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n                        );\n                        return Response::builder()\n                .status(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS)\n                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                .unwrap();\n                    }\n                    // Allowed, proceed\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n            &source_ip,\n            limit,\n            window_seconds * 1000,\n        );\n        if !allowed {\n            warn!(\n                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n            );\n            return Response::builder()\n                .status(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS)\n                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                .unwrap();\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n    let (pinned_group, _strategy_label) = if let Some(sid) = session_id {\n        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid.as_str()).await {\n            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n            match strategy {\n                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n                        sid.as_str(),\n                        &state.task_registry,\n                        max_wait,\n                    ).await {\n                        Ok(true) => {\n                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n                        }\n                        Ok(false) => {\n                            // Write failed, clear pin\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n                        }\n                        Err(_) => {\n                            // Timeout - route to pinned group (best effort)\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (Some(group), None::<u64>)\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n                    (Some(group), None::<u64>)\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // No pending write - normal routing\n            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n        }\n    } else {\n        // No session header - normal routing\n        (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n    };\n\n    // For multi-target aliases, we use the first target for settings/scoped key\n    // All targets should have compatible settings (managed by ILM)\n    let primary_target = match targets.first() {\n        Some(t) => t,\n        None => {\n            error!(\"multi-target alias has no targets\");\n            return Response::builder()\n                .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                .unwrap();\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Get the scoped key for the primary target\n    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(primary_target) {\n            // Refresh scoped-key beacon\n            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n                &state.pod_id,\n                primary_target,\n                sk.generation,\n            );\n            sk.primary_key\n        } else {\n            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n    };\n\n    // Use live topology from shared state\n    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n        _ => return Response::builder()\n            .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n            .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n            .unwrap(),\n    };\n\n    // Plan scatter for primary target (for ILM read aliases, all targets\n    // should have identical shard layout)\n    let plan = {\n        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n            \"scatter_plan_multi_target\",\n            primary_target = %primary_target,\n            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n            shards = state.config.shards,\n            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n            min_settings_version,\n            pinned_group = ?pinned_group,\n            target_count = targets.len(),\n        ).entered();\n\n        if let Some(group) = pinned_group {\n            match plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                group,\n                None,\n            ).await {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    warn!(\n                        pinned_group = group,\n                        \"pinned group unavailable, falling back to normal routing\"\n                    );\n                    plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, None).await\n                }\n            }\n        } else if let Some(floor) = min_settings_version {\n            let settings_broadcast = state.settings_broadcast.clone();\n            let plan_result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                primary_target,\n                floor,\n                &move |idx, node_id| {\n                    let sb = settings_broadcast.clone();\n                    let idx = idx.to_string();\n                    let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n                    tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n                        tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n                            sb.node_version(&idx, &node_id).await\n                        })\n                    })\n                },\n                None,\n            ).await;\n\n            match plan_result {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    let _err = MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n                        format!(\n                            \"no covering set available for settings version floor {} on index '{}'\",\n                            floor, primary_target\n                        ),\n                    );\n                    return Response::builder()\n                        .status(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)\n                        .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                        .unwrap();\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, None).await\n        }\n    };\n    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n\n    // Record scatter fan-out size\n    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n\n    // Build search request for primary target\n    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: primary_target.to_string(),\n        query: body.q.clone(),\n        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n        filter: body.filter.clone(),\n        facets: body.facets.clone(),\n        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n        body: body.rest.clone(),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    // Create node client\n    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n        search_key,\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    ));\n    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone(), None);\n\n    // Use score-based merge strategy\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n    // Execute search\n    let mut result = match dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        search_req,\n        &topo,\n        policy,\n        &strategy,\n    )\n    .await\n    {\n        Ok(r) => r,\n        Err(e) => {\n            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"multi-target search failed\");\n            return Response::builder()\n                .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                .unwrap();\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Drop topology lock before building response\n    drop(topo);\n\n    // Strip internal fields from hits\n    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n    }\n\n    // Build response body\n    let mut response_body = serde_json::json!({\n        \"hits\": result.hits,\n        \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n        \"processingTimeMs\": result.processing_time_ms,\n    });\n\n    // Only include facetDistribution if facets were requested\n    if let Some(facets) = &result.facet_distribution {\n        response_body[\"facetDistribution\"] = serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    }\n\n    // Build response with optional headers\n    let mut response = Response::builder()\n        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n\n    // Add settings headers\n    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(primary_target).await {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n    }\n\n    let current_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n    if current_version > 0 {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", current_version.to_string());\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded {\n        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n        let mut sorted_shards = result.failed_shards.clone();\n        sorted_shards.sort();\n        let shard_ids = sorted_shards.iter()\n            .map(|id| id.to_string())\n            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n            .join(\",\");\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids));\n    } else if result.degraded {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", \"partial\");\n    }\n\n    let response = response\n        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&response_body).unwrap()))\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Structured log entry\n    tracing::info!(\n        target: \"miroir.search_multi_target\",\n        primary_target = %primary_target,\n        target_count = targets.len(),\n        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n        node_count = node_count,\n        estimated_hits = result.estimated_total_hits,\n        degraded = result.degraded,\n        \"multi-target search completed\"\n    );\n\n    response\n}\n\n/// Strip `_miroir_shard` from all hits (always).\n/// Strip `_rankingScore` unless the client explicitly requested it.\npub fn strip_internal_fields(hit: &mut Value, client_requested_score: bool) {\n    if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n        obj.remove(\"_miroir_shard\");\n        if !client_requested_score {\n            obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_request_debug_redaction() {\n        let body = SearchRequestBody {\n            q: Some(\"sensitive user query about private data\".to_string()),\n            offset: Some(0),\n            limit: Some(20),\n            filter: Some(serde_json::json!({\"email\": Search route handler with DFS (Distributed Frequency Search) support.\n\nuse axum::extract::{Extension, Path};\nuse axum::http::{HeaderMap, StatusCode};\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, miroir_core::idempotency::QueryFingerprint;\nuse miroir_core::merger::ScoreMergeStrategy;\nuse miroir_core::replica_selection::SelectionObserver;\nuse miroir_core::scatter::{\n    dfs_query_then_fetch_search, plan_search_scatter, plan_search_scatter_for_group, plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor, SearchRequest, NodeClient,\n};\nuse
